records.ety: [Originally sent, fr. F. sentir to feel, to smell. See Sense.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Scented; p. pr. & vb. n. Scenting.]

defs: To perceive by the olfactory organs; to smell; as, to scent game, as a hound does.
quotes: Methinks I scent the morning air.

defs: To imbue or fill with odor; to perfume.
quotes: Balm from a silver box distilled around, Shall all bedew the roots, and scent the sacred ground.
Thunderbolts . . . do scent strongly of brimstone.

defs: To hunt animals by means of the sense of smell.
That which, issuing from a body, affects the olfactory organs of animals; odor; smell; as, the scent of an orange, or of a rose; the scent of musk.
quotes: With lavish hand diffuses scents ambrosial.

defs: Specifically, the odor left by an animal on the ground in passing over it; as, dogs find or lose the scent; hence, course of pursuit; track of discovery.
quotes: He gained the observations of innumerable ages, and traveled upon the same scent into Ethiopia.

defs: The power of smelling; the sense of smell; as, a hound of nice scent; to divert the scent.
